atmosphere.mood: Rough and loud. Strangers are welcome if they have coin.
atmosphere.lighting: Dim — lanterns swing with the sea draft, shadows everywhere
atmosphere.smells: Salt air, fish, tar, and something unidentifiable burning in the firepit
atmosphere.sounds: Shouted arguments, dice rattling on wood, boots on wet planking, distant seagulls

layout: A low-ceilinged room with heavy beams. Long communal tables, benches worn smooth by decades of sailors. A bar running the full length of one wall. A locked door at the back that nobody asks about.

drinks.name: Ship's grog
drinks.description: Rum cut with water and lime. Barely.

drinks.name: Dark rum
drinks.description: Imported from the southern islands — molasses-sweet and fierce

drinks.name: Foreign wine
drinks.description: A rough bottle of something from distant shores, likely traded off a ship

drinks.name: Sailor's tot
drinks.description: A stiff measure of spirit dispensed at the end of a hard watch

drinks.name: Water
drinks.description: A cup of well water, clear and cold

food.name: Salt cod
food.description: Shredded and pan-fried with onions — a staple of the dockside diet

food.name: Shellfish stew
food.description: Mussels, whelks, and clams in a briny broth — if it smells off, send it back
